2026-03-15T01:46:37.3168455Z       - At close range, this causes aggressive lane changes and wrong-way driving
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3168957Z       - MISSION_34 (FollowCarFaraway) is more conservative, reducing this effect

2026-03-15T01:46:37.3174731Z               {

2026-03-15T01:46:37.3177013Z

2026-03-15T01:46:37.3186804Z       
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3187008Z       **Behavior Summary**:
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3187314Z       - When recruit goes offroad (>28m from road node):
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3187678Z         1. CIVICO mode snaps to road-graph immediately
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3188077Z         2. NO fallback to GOTOCOORDS+PLOUGH_THROUGH (NO beelining)
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3188453Z         3. Keeps snap timer at 0 to force re-snap on exit
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3188806Z         4. Maintains CIVICO mission (43 or 34) while offroad
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3189104Z

2026-03-15T01:46:37.3189652Z       ## 4. INVALID_LINK GUARD (lines 415-464)
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3189910Z       
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3190092Z       ### What is INVALID_LINK?
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3190568Z       - `linkId > 50000` (MAX_VALID_LINK_ID)
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3190930Z       - Can be produced by JoinCarWithRoadSystem in certain positions
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3191513Z       - When invalid: ClipTargetOrientationToLink returns garbage → WRONG_DIR
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3191934Z       - Causes sidewalk driving, wrong lanes
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3192196Z       
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3192381Z       ### Guard Logic (lines 424-464):
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3192820Z       ```cpp
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3192996Z       {
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3193304Z           unsigned linkId = (unsigned)ap.m_nCurrentPathNodeInfo.m_nCarPathLinkId;
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3193832Z           if (linkId > MAX_VALID_LINK_ID)  // 50000
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3194106Z           {
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3194298Z               if (!g_wasInvalidLink)
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3194535Z               {
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3194740Z                   g_wasInvalidLink = true;
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3195040Z                   // FIRST DETECTION: Try immediate re-snap
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3195461Z                   LogDrive("INVALID_LINK: linkId=%u (INVALID! MAX=%u) - immediate re-snap "
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3195907Z                            "(no beelining)", linkId, MAX_VALID_LINK_ID);
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3196260Z                   CCarCtrl::JoinCarWithRoadSystem(veh);
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3196565Z                   g_civicRoadSnapTimer = 0;
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3196927Z                   linkId = (unsigned)ap.m_nCurrentPathNodeInfo.m_nCarPathLinkId;
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3197287Z                   
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3197506Z                   if (linkId <= MAX_VALID_LINK_ID)
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3197770Z                   {
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3197980Z                       // Re-snap fixed it!
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3198383Z                       LogDrive("INVALID_LINK: re-snap fixed -> linkId=%u, CIVICO restored", linkId);
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3198820Z                       g_wasInvalidLink = false;
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3199077Z                   }
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3199269Z                   else
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3199464Z                   {
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3199679Z                       // Still invalid after re-snap
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3200046Z                       LogDrive("INVALID_LINK: re-snap still invalid -> linkId=%u, "
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3200737Z                                "CIVICO reduced (periodic snap will fix)", linkId);
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3201062Z                   }
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3201252Z               }
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3201438Z               
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3201634Z               if (g_wasInvalidLink)
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3201870Z               {
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3202192Z                   // Invalid state continues: reduce speed but stay in CIVICO (NO beelining)
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3202629Z                   ap.m_nCruiseSpeed = SPEED_SLOW;  // 12 km/h
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3202990Z                   // Periodic snap below will correct it within 5 seconds
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3203299Z               }
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3203476Z           }
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3203670Z           else if (g_wasInvalidLink)
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3203908Z           {
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3204099Z               // Link became valid again
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3204473Z               LogDrive("INVALID_LINK: linkId=%u - link valid restored, resume CIVICO "
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3204890Z                        "and re-snap road-graph", linkId);
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3205184Z               g_wasInvalidLink = false;
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3205476Z               CCarCtrl::JoinCarWithRoadSystem(veh);
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3205769Z               g_civicRoadSnapTimer = 0;
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3206026Z           }
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3206193Z       }
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3206363Z       ```
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3206533Z       
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3206707Z       **Key Points**:
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3207016Z       - Does NOT fall back to GOTOCOORDS+PLOUGH_THROUGH (this was removed)
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3207445Z       - Reduces speed to 12 km/h to minimize damage while waiting
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3207795Z       - Periodic snap (every 5s) will correct it
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3208102Z       - Prevents ~28 seconds of sidewalk beelining
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3208369Z       
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3208536Z       ---
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3208702Z       
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3208899Z       ## 5. PERIODIC ROAD SNAP (lines 534-554)
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3209160Z       
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3209402Z       ### When: Every ~5 seconds (300 frames @ 60fps) in CIVICO mode
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3209883Z       ### Only: When on-road (not offroad)
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3210128Z       
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3210459Z       **Code** (lines 538-554):
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3210682Z       ```cpp

2026-03-15T01:46:37.3211299Z       {
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3211553Z           if (++g_civicRoadSnapTimer >= ROAD_SNAP_INTERVAL)  // 300 frames
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3211878Z           {
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3212066Z               g_civicRoadSnapTimer = 0;
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3212463Z               unsigned linkBefore = (unsigned)ap.m_nCurrentPathNodeInfo.m_nCarPathLinkId;
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3212896Z               CCarCtrl::JoinCarWithRoadSystem(veh);
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3213304Z               unsigned linkAfter = (unsigned)ap.m_nCurrentPathNodeInfo.m_nCarPathLinkId;
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3213765Z               LogDrive("PERIODIC_ROAD_SNAP: dist=%.1fm linkId %u->%u "
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3214125Z                        "(re-snap road-graph every %ds)",
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3214477Z                   dist, linkBefore, linkAfter, ROAD_SNAP_INTERVAL / 60);
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3214787Z           }
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3214955Z       }
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3215124Z       else
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3215304Z       {
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3215529Z           // Exit CIVICO or entered offroad: reset snap timer
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3215840Z           g_civicRoadSnapTimer = 0;
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3216079Z       }
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3216241Z       ```
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3216408Z       
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3216579Z       **Purpose**: 
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3216866Z       - Maintain alignment with road nodes during continuous driving
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3217247Z       - Reduce accumulated lane deviations over time
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3217560Z       - Corrects INVALID_LINK within 5 seconds
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3217818Z       
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3217989Z       ---

2026-03-15T01:46:37.3235897Z       ProcessDrivingAI(player)
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3236211Z       ├─ If dist < 6m: STOP_ZONE
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3236513Z       │  └─ mission = STOP_FOREVER, speed = 0
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3236819Z       │     return (skip rest)
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3237047Z       │
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3237265Z       ├─ If 6m ≤ dist < 10m: SLOW_ZONE
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3237545Z       │  ├─ speed = 12 km/h
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3237835Z       │  ├─ If CIVICO: restore expected mission
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3238136Z       │  └─ return (skip rest)
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3238365Z       │
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3238579Z       ├─ If dist ≥ 10m: NORMAL_ZONE
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3238886Z       │  ├─ Offroad detection (throttled 0.5s)
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3239244Z       │  │  └─ If CIVICO + entering offroad: snap to road
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3239553Z       │  │
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3239820Z       │  ├─ If DIRETO mode: update destination, return
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3240132Z       │  │
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3240480Z       │  ├─ If PARADO mode: return
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3240731Z       │  │

2026-03-15T01:46:37.3241226Z       │  │

2026-03-15T01:46:37.3249574Z       | Constant | Value | Purpose |
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3249879Z       |----------|-------|---------|
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3250162Z       | STOP_ZONE_M | 6m | Recruit stops completely |
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3250625Z       | SLOW_ZONE_M | 10m | Recruit slows down (12 km/h) |
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3250987Z       | MEDIUM_DIST_M | 25m | CIVICO_D auto-degrade threshold |
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3251369Z       | OFFROAD_DIST_M | 28m | Distance to nearest node = offroad |
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3251731Z       | SPEED_CIVICO | 38 km/h | Normal driving speed |
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3252066Z       | SPEED_SLOW | 12 km/h | In SLOW_ZONE or INVALID_LINK |
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3252453Z       | ROAD_SNAP_INTERVAL | 300 frames (5s) | Periodic snap frequency |
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3252907Z       | OFFROAD_CHECK_INTERVAL | 30 frames (0.5s) | Offroad detection throttle |

2026-03-15T01:46:37.3253659Z       
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3253827Z       ---
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3254000Z       
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3254188Z       ## 9. OFF-ROAD BEHAVIOR SUMMARY
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3254433Z       
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3254619Z       ### When does recruit go off-road?
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3254923Z       - **Trigger**: Distance to nearest road node > 28m
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3255247Z       - **Detection**: Throttled every 0.5 seconds
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3255511Z       
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3255727Z       ### What happens when going off-road near player?
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3256050Z       1. Detected via DetectOffroad() (line 32-48)
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3256407Z       2. In CIVICO mode only: immediate JoinCarWithRoadSystem snap
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3256812Z       3. NO fallback to GOTOCOORDS+PLOUGH_THROUGH (beelining)
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3257153Z       4. Continues with CIVICO mission (43 or 34)
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3257471Z       5. Snap timer reset to force re-snap on road exit
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3257743Z       
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3257955Z       ### Why not DIRETO (GOTOCOORDS+PLOUGH_THROUGH)?
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3258256Z       - Was previously causing:
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3258495Z         - Sidewalk climbing
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3258724Z         - Wrong lane driving
2026-03-15T01:46:37.3258978Z         - Aggressive beelining for ~28 seconds

2026-03-15T01:47:10.9109447Z ### **9. ROOT CAUSE: SIDEWALK CLIMBING IN CIVICO MODE**
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9109813Z 
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9110122Z **Problem**: Recruit climbs sidewalks/wrong lanes when close to player
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9110776Z 
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9110912Z **Root Cause**: 
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9111450Z - **MISSION_43 (EscortRearFaraway)** copies player heading frame-by-frame
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9112280Z - At close range (especially < 25m), causes aggressive heading changes
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9113039Z - Results in wrong-way turns, lane violations, sidewalk climbing
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9113480Z 
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9113633Z **Solutions Implemented**:
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9114233Z 1. **Auto-degrade to MISSION_34** (dist < 25m): Switches to less aggressive mission
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9115127Z 2. **INVALID_LINK guard**: Detects & fixes malformed road links, prevents beelining
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9115989Z 3. **Periodic snap (5s)**: Maintains alignment with road network
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9116957Z 4. **OFFROAD snap**: Immediately re-aligns if going off-road (no beelining)
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9117459Z 
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9117574Z ---
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9117736Z

2026-03-15T01:47:10.9174994Z     ### **2. AUTO-DEGRADE SYSTEM (Lines 505-532) - PRIMARY FIX FOR SIDEWALK ISSUE**
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9175632Z     
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9176115Z     **ONLY in CIVICO_D mode**, switches missions based on distance to player:
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9176726Z     
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9177016Z     ```cpp

2026-03-15T01:47:10.9177944Z     {

2026-03-15T01:47:10.9179178Z         if (ap.m_nCarMission != idealMission)
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9179617Z         {
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9180108Z             if (ap.m_nCarMission == MISSION_43 || ap.m_nCarMission == MISSION_34)
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9180880Z             {

2026-03-15T01:47:10.9182023Z                     dist,
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9182528Z                     (dist < MEDIUM_DIST_M) ? "degrade(perto:menos_chase)" : 
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9183141Z                     "restore(longe:road_follow)",
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9183601Z                     ...);
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9184022Z                 ap.m_nCarMission = idealMission;
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9184531Z                 ap.m_pTargetCar  = playerCar;

2026-03-15T01:47:10.9185277Z         }
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9185566Z     }
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9185848Z     ```
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9186123Z     
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9186425Z     **Distance Logic**:
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9187201Z     - **dist < 25m** → MISSION_34 (FollowCarFaraway) = less aggressive, no "chase" behavior
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9188207Z     - **dist ≥ 25m** → MISSION_43 (EscortRearFaraway) = standard road-following
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9188813Z     
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9189141Z     **Why This Fixes Sidewalk Climbing**:
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9189779Z     - MISSION_43 copies player heading frame-by-frame (very aggressive)
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9190996Z     - At close range, causes aggressive lane changes and wrong-way turns
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9191792Z     - MISSION_34 is more conservative, preventing sidewalk/lane violations
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9192401Z     
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9192870Z     ---
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9193123Z

2026-03-15T01:47:10.9195435Z     {
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9195749Z         bool wasOffroad = g_isOffroad;
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9196371Z         g_isOffroad = DetectOffroad(veh);  // distance to nearest node > 28m?
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9197016Z         g_offroadTimer = OFFROAD_CHECK_INTERVAL;  // 30 frames
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9197438Z         
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9197779Z         if (g_isOffroad != wasOffroad)
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9198209Z         {
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9198576Z             // WHEN ENTERING OFFROAD IN CIVICO MODE:
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9199134Z             if (g_isOffroad && IsCivicoMode(g_driveMode))
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9199611Z             {
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9200013Z                 CCarCtrl::JoinCarWithRoadSystem(veh);
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9200799Z                 g_civicRoadSnapTimer = 0;
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9201401Z                 LogDrive("OFFROAD_ENTER_SNAP: JoinCarWithRoadSystem");
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9201972Z             }
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9202273Z         }
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9202562Z     }
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9202844Z     
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9203150Z     // WHILE OFFROAD IN CIVICO:
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9203619Z     if (g_isOffroad && IsCivicoMode(g_driveMode))
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9204077Z     {
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9204466Z         g_civicRoadSnapTimer = 0;  // Force re-snap on exit
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9204975Z     }
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9205249Z     ```
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9205540Z     
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9205888Z     **When recruit goes off-road near player**:
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9206421Z     1. Detected when > 28m from nearest road node
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9206939Z     2. Immediately snaps to road network
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9207520Z     3. **NO fallback to GOTOCOORDS+PLOUGH_THROUGH** (no beelining)
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9208121Z     4. Maintains CIVICO mission (43 or 34)
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9208593Z     5. Forces re-snap when exiting offroad
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9209025Z     
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9209305Z     ---
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9209587Z     
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9210024Z     ### **4. INVALID_LINK GUARD (Lines 415-464) - SECONDARY PROTECTION**
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9210802Z     
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9211082Z     ```cpp

2026-03-15T01:47:10.9230349Z         CCarCtrl::JoinCarWithRoadSystem(veh);
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9230869Z         g_civicRoadSnapTimer = 0;
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9231277Z     }
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9231564Z     ```
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9231852Z     
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9232156Z     **Key Protection**:
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9232610Z     - Detects malformed road links (linkId > 50000)
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9233101Z     - Tries immediate re-snap
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9233635Z     - If still invalid: reduces speed to 12 km/h, stays in CIVICO (no beelining)
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9234308Z     - Periodic snap (5s) will correct it
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9234905Z     - **Prevents previous ~28 second sidewalk beelining bug**
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9235453Z

2026-03-15T01:47:10.9239414Z             g_civicRoadSnapTimer = 0;
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9240115Z             unsigned linkBefore = (unsigned)ap.m_nCurrentPathNodeInfo.m_nCarPathLinkId;
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9241282Z             CCarCtrl::JoinCarWithRoadSystem(veh);

2026-03-15T01:47:10.9243870Z     }

2026-03-15T01:47:10.9244262Z     {

2026-03-15T01:47:10.9245215Z     ```
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9245409Z     
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9245608Z     **Purpose**:
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9245902Z     - Every 5 seconds, re-align with road network nodes
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9246282Z     - Reduce accumulated lane deviations
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9246624Z     - Correct INVALID_LINK state within cycle
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9246991Z     - Only in CIVICO mode and on-road (not offroad)

2026-03-15T01:47:10.9265903Z     ### **7. COMPLETE ProcessDrivingAI FLOW**
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9266159Z     
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9266313Z     ```
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9266492Z     ProcessDrivingAI(player)
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9266770Z     │
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9266993Z     ├─ dist < 6m: STOP_ZONE
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9267305Z     │  └─ mission=STOP_FOREVER, speed=0 → RETURN
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9267585Z     │
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9267802Z     ├─ 6m ≤ dist < 10m: SLOW_ZONE  
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9268076Z     │  ├─ speed=12km/h
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9268358Z     │  ├─ If CIVICO: restore expected mission
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9268923Z     │  └─ RETURN (no periodic snap here anymore!)
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9269192Z     │
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9269400Z     ├─ dist ≥ 10m: NORMAL_ZONE
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9269633Z     │  │
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9270044Z     │  ├─ Offroad detection (throttled 0.5s)
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9270558Z     │  │  └─ If CIVICO+offroad: snap to road (no beelining)
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9270871Z     │  │
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9271116Z     │  ├─ If DIRETO: update destination → RETURN
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9271391Z     │  │
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9271596Z     │  ├─ If PARADO: RETURN
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9271816Z     │  │
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9272022Z     │  └─ [CIVICO MODE FROM HERE]
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9272266Z     │     │
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9272503Z     │     ├─ Ensure driveStyle = STOP_FOR_CARS
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9272778Z     │     │
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9272991Z     │     ├─ INVALID_LINK guard
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9273274Z     │     │  ├─ If linkId > 50000: try re-snap
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9273611Z     │     │  └─ If still bad: reduce speed to 12km/h
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9273908Z     │     │
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9274116Z     │     ├─ MISSION_RECOVERY
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9274434Z     │     │  └─ If mission=STOP_FOREVER: restore to expected
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9274744Z     │     │

2026-03-15T01:47:10.9275297Z     │     │  ├─ dist < 25m: MISSION_34
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9275582Z     │     │  └─ dist ≥ 25m: MISSION_43
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9275840Z     │     │

2026-03-15T01:47:10.9276696Z     │     │
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9276910Z     │     ├─ Adaptive speed in curves
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9277250Z     │     │  └─ Reduce via straightLineDistance multiplier
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9277559Z     │     │
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9277787Z     │     └─ WRONG_DIR detection & logging
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9278032Z     ```

2026-03-15T01:47:10.9278695Z     ### **8. KEY CONSTANTS**
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9278912Z     
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9279068Z     ```cpp
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9279259Z     // Distance zones (meters)
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9279580Z     static constexpr float STOP_ZONE_M   = 6.0f;    // Completely stop
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9280106Z     static constexpr float SLOW_ZONE_M   = 10.0f;   // Slow down to 12 km/h
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9281066Z     static constexpr float MEDIUM_DIST_M = 25.0f;   // CIVICO_D auto-degrade threshold
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9281600Z     static constexpr float OFFROAD_DIST_M = 28.0f;  // Distance to node = offroad
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9281967Z     
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9282201Z     // Speeds (SA units ≈ km/h)
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9282558Z     static constexpr unsigned char SPEED_CIVICO = 38;   // Normal road-following
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9283095Z     static constexpr unsigned char SPEED_SLOW   = 12;   // In SLOW_ZONE or INVALID_LINK
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9283502Z     
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9283682Z     // Intervals (frames @ 60 fps)
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9283995Z     static constexpr int OFFROAD_CHECK_INTERVAL = 30;   // 0.5s
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9284391Z     static constexpr int ROAD_SNAP_INTERVAL     = 300;  // 5.0s
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9284694Z     
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9284862Z     // Link validation
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9285127Z     static constexpr unsigned MAX_VALID_LINK_ID  = 50000u;
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9285423Z     ```
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9285583Z     
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9285746Z     ---
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9285903Z     
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9286123Z     ### **9. ROOT CAUSE: SIDEWALK CLIMBING IN CIVICO MODE**
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9286404Z     
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9286672Z     **Problem**: Recruit climbs sidewalks/wrong lanes when close to player
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9287012Z     
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9287176Z     **Root Cause**: 
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9287499Z     - **MISSION_43 (EscortRearFaraway)** copies player heading frame-by-frame
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9288132Z     - At close range (especially < 25m), causes aggressive heading changes
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9288569Z     - Results in wrong-way turns, lane violations, sidewalk climbing
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9288899Z     
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9289179Z     **Solutions Implemented**:
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9289546Z     1. **Auto-degrade to MISSION_34** (dist < 25m): Switches to less aggressive mission
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9290066Z     2. **INVALID_LINK guard**: Detects & fixes malformed road links, prevents beelining
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9290836Z     3. **Periodic snap (5s)**: Maintains alignment with road network
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9291286Z     4. **OFFROAD snap**: Immediately re-aligns if going off-road (no beelining)
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9291641Z     
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9291802Z     ---
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9291966Z     
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9292139Z     ### **10. SUMMARY TABLE**
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9292356Z     
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9292541Z     | Aspect | Value/Behavior |
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9292787Z     |--------|-----------------|
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9293073Z     | **When goes off-road** | > 28m from nearest road node |
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9293505Z     | **Off-road in CIVICO** | Snaps to road, maintains CIVICO mission (no beelining) |
2026-03-15T01:47:10.9294088Z     | **Behavior change at dist < 25m** | CIVICO_D switches from MISSION_43 to MISSION_34 (less aggressive) |

2026-03-15T01:50:51.5227629Z Looking at GetSimplestActiveTask, it retrieves the first non-null task from the primary tasks array starting at index 0, which means it prioritizes lower-indexed slots over higher ones.
2026-03-15T01:50:51.5228338Z 
2026-03-15T01:50:51.5229724Z When GANG_SPAWN_COMPLEX occupies slot[2], GetSimplestActiveTask dives through its subtask chain and returns the deepest one (GANG_SPAWN_AI at 400). For GANG_FOLLOWER to be returned instead, it would need to be positioned at a lower slot index than STAND_STILL.
2026-03-15T01:50:51.5231380Z

2026-03-15T01:50:51.5235170Z

Yet the slot dump clearly shows slot[3] as empty with NO_TASK(-1), which means GANG_FOLLOWER isn't actually there at all.

2026-03-15T01:51:54.5692265Z - [4] = DEFAULT (default/simple task)
2026-03-15T01:51:54.5692592Z 
2026-03-15T01:51:54.5693179Z GANG_FOLLOWER (a complex task) would go to slot[3] (PRIMARY), while STAND_STILL (simple task) is at slot[4] (DEFAULT).
2026-03-15T01:51:54.5693955Z 
2026-03-15T01:51:54.5694130Z ### 2. CTaskComplexGangFollower:
2026-03-15T01:51:54.5695281Z The constructor takes a ped group pointer from the global groups manager, the ped itself, and positioning parameters to set up the gang follower behavior.
2026-03-15T01:51:54.5696109Z 
2026-03-15T01:51:54.5696253Z ### 3. GANG_FOLLOWER never assigned:
2026-03-15T01:51:54.5698133Z Looking at the execution trace, the ped successfully joins the group at frame 692, but despite the follow command firing at frame 693 and repeatedly triggering the fallback at frame 695+, the GANG_FOLLOWER task never actually gets placed into the primary slot—the ped is in the group but the task assignment isn't happening.
2026-03-15T01:51:54.5699535Z 
2026-03-15T01:51:54.5702557Z I'm tracing through `GroupIntelComputeDefaultTasks()` and seeing that it clears existing default task pairs then calls the allocator's `AllocateDefaultTasks()`, which populates `m_DefaultPedTaskPairs` rather than directly assigning to the ped's task slots. These default pairs only get applied when `CPedGroupIntelligence::Process()` runs, which requires the group to be "active"—meaning the leader needs to be nearby and not in a cutscene or similar state. If the player is the leader and active, Process() should execute each frame, but something's preventing the default tasks from being applied to the recruit.
2026-03-15T01:51:54.5705758Z 
2026-03-15T01:51:54.5708642Z Wait, the function description says "IMMEDIATELY" apply the default tasks, which suggests it shouldn't wait for Process() to run. So either 0x5F88D0 is just writing to `m_DefaultPedTaskPairs` without the immediate application step, or there's something else blocking the task assignment from actually reaching the ped's task manager. The comment suggests this function should apply tasks immediately after SetDefaultTaskAllocatorType, but the logs show it's not working as expected. I'm going to try a different approach entirely—directly setting a CTaskComplexGangFollower task, which takes the ped group and the follower ped as constructor parameters.
2026-03-15T01:51:54.5712346Z 
2026-03-15T01:51:54.5715036Z Looking at the constructor, it also needs a position vector that gets stored as m_PedPosn, which CalculateOffsetPosition() might use to determine where the follower should stand relative to the leader. I'm not entirely sure if this should be the ped's current position, the leader's position, or just a zero vector—the task will likely recalculate it anyway when CreateFirstSubTask runs.
2026-03-15T01:51:54.5717436Z 
2026-03-15T01:51:54.5718115Z The real question is what ControlSubTask does with m_PedPosn and whether that's where the positioning logic actually breaks down.
2026-03-15T01:51:54.5719220Z 
2026-03-15T01:51:54.5721131Z For now, I'll pass the player's position as a reasonable default, since that's where the follower should be targeting anyway. Looking at the gta-reversed comments, the constructor sets up the initial following position, so passing the leader's current position makes sense.
2026-03-15T01:51:54.5722859Z 
2026-03-15T01:51:54.5725037Z For the task slot, GANG_FOLLOWER is complex enough that it should go into TASK_PRIMARY_PRIMARY at slot index 3, though I need to check what slot our code is actually using. TASK_PRIMARY_DEFAULT (slot 4), which is the "default" background task slot in GTA SA. So when the gang follower is told to follow the player, STAND_STILL gets placed in that default slot rather than the primary task slot.
2026-03-15T01:51:54.5727462Z 
2026-03-15T01:51:54.5730604Z Now I'm tracing through the call chain: `TellGroupToStartFollowingPlayer` → `ComputeDefaultTasks` → `AllocateDefaultTasks` → `CTaskAllocatorGangFollower::AllocateTasks`, and it looks like the task manager sets the task using `SetTask(task, TASK_PRIMARY_DEFAULT, false)`. This means `CTaskComplexGangFollower` should also end up in slot 4 as the default behavior, replacing STAND_STILL.
2026-03-15T01:51:54.5733070Z 
2026-03-15T01:51:54.5734490Z Looking at the `SetTask` implementation, it calls `ChangeTaskInSlot` which always replaces the old task with the new one regardless of the `forceReset` parameter. So even though `false` is passed, STAND_STILL should get replaced by the gang follower task.
2026-03-15T01:51:54.5736120Z 
2026-03-15T01:51:54.5740793Z Since that's not happening, I'm considering three possibilities: either the allocator is writing to a different slot, or it's writing to the default task pairs which get applied later but something's blocking that application, or `AllocateDefaultTasks` isn't being called for this ped at all. Looking more closely at `ComputeDefaultTasks`, it clears the default task pairs and then calls the allocator to populate them, but the actual assignment to the ped's task manager happens somewhere in the `CP` method—which is where the issue likely lies. edGroupIntelligence::ApplyDefaultTasks()` or similar, which is called from `Process()`.
2026-03-15T01:51:54.5742955Z 
2026-03-15T01:51:54.5743059Z So the timeline would be:
2026-03-15T01:51:54.5743410Z 1. We call `SetAllocatorType(4)` + `ComputeDefaultTasks(pIntel, g_recruit)`
2026-03-15T01:51:54.5743912Z 2. This writes GANG_FOLLOWER to `m_DefaultPedTaskPairs[i]` for g_recruit
2026-03-15T01:51:54.5744416Z 3. Next frame, `CPedGroupIntelligence::Process()` calls `ApplyDefaultTasks()`
2026-03-15T01:51:54.5744997Z 4. `ApplyDefaultTasks()` calls `ped->m_pIntelligence->m_TaskMgr.SetTask(gangFollower, slot, false)`
2026-03-15T01:51:54.5745601Z 5. But step 4 NEVER happens → GANG_FOLLOWER never appears
2026-03-15T01:51:54.5745832Z 
2026-03-15T01:51:54.5745950Z Why does step 4 never happen? Possibilities:
2026-03-15T01:51:54.5746285Z a) `CPedGroupIntelligence::Process()` doesn't run
2026-03-15T01:51:54.5747026Z b) Process() runs but `ApplyDefaultTasks()` is gated on some condition
2026-03-15T01:51:54.5747881Z c) Process() runs and ApplyDefaultTasks() runs, but after setting the task, something immediately clears it
2026-03-15T01:51:54.5748318Z 
2026-03-15T01:51:54.5750941Z I'm wondering if `CPedGroupIntelligence::Process()` even executes at all. Since it's part of `CPedGroup` which gets processed by `CPedGroups::Process()` in the main game loop, it should run every frame. But maybe there's a check for a valid leader—the player is in slot[0] of m_groupMembership, so if that's valid, the group should process normally. Or possibly `m_DefaultTaskAllocator->AllocateDefaultTasks()` has its own conditions that prevent it from running.
2026-03-15T01:51:54.5753570Z 
2026-03-15T01:51:54.5755848Z The allocator for type 4 (FollowLeaderAnyMeans) might skip default task assignment if the ped is in a critical state like a gang fight, which could trigger for a GSF ped in GSF territory. Alternatively, the task gets set correctly but then `ProcessTasks()` immediately runs on the new GANG_FOLLOWER task, and its `CreateFirstSubTask` returns STAND_STILL, effectively overwriting what we just set.
2026-03-15T01:51:54.5758501Z 
2026-03-15T01:51:54.5760388Z I'm realizing the issue might be in how `BuildPrimaryTaskBuf` retrieves the task ID—it might not be calling `GetId()` directly on the slot's task but on something else entirely. I need to examine the actual implementation in Main.cpp to figure out what's happening.

2026-03-15T01:51:54.5893829Z     377.     case ModState::INACTIVE:

2026-03-15T01:53:30.6810612Z

2026-03-15T01:53:30.6812272Z 
2026-03-15T01:53:30.6814892Z Setting up the direct task assignment... I'm realizing there's a problem with using `operator new` directly—it allocates from our plugin's runtime heap, but GTA SA uses its own memory management system with custom vtables. I need to call the game's own `operator new` function at the specific address to ensure the object gets allocated correctly on the game's heap. the task is later deleted by the game engine (`ChangeTaskInSlot` calls `delete task`), it would use the GAME's `operator delete`, causing a mismatch crash if the memory was allocated with a different allocator!
2026-03-15T01:53:30.6817352Z 
2026-03-15T01:53:30.6817749Z This is a serious issue. We need to allocate the task memory using the same allocator that the game uses.
2026-03-15T01:53:30.6818357Z 
2026-03-15T01:53:30.6818516Z Looking at how gta-reversed handles this in plugins:
2026-03-15T01:53:30.6819214Z - They use `plugin::CallMethod<0x65EAA0>` which calls the game's constructor via the game's memory management
2026-03-15T01:53:30.6820338Z - The constructor at 0x65EAA0 uses `CTaskComplex::CTaskComplex()` base constructor which presumably uses the game's vtable
2026-03-15T01:53:30.6821224Z 
2026-03-15T01:53:30.6822681Z Actually wait - in C++ plugins that inject into games, if we do `new CTaskComplexGangFollower(...)`, the allocation goes through our plugin's `operator new`. When the game later tries to `delete task`, it uses the GAME's `operator delete`. This IS a crash-prone pattern.
2026-03-15T01:53:30.6823697Z 
2026-03-15T01:53:30.6825338Z But looking at the existing code that creates a task with `new` and passes it to the game's task manager - this would normally be a bug when the game eventually deletes it, unless the plugin-sdk patches the game's allocators to be compatible with the CRT heap, or the game's heap manager is already compatible.
2026-03-15T01:53:30.6826541Z 
2026-03-15T01:53:30.6826731Z Checking plugin-sdk...
2026-03-15T01:53:30.6826941Z 
2026-03-15T01:53:30.6828166Z Actually, the simplest solution would be to just include the header and use the normal constructor, but the plugin-sdk doesn't have `CTaskComplexGangFollower.h` available. So I need to find another way to allocate and construct this task using the game's memory manager directly. Create our own minimal stub class that has the right vtable and size, and call the constructor at 0x65EAA0.
2026-03-15T01:53:30.6829482Z 
2026-03-15T01:53:30.6829674Z Actually, the cleanest way (and how plugin modders typically do this) is:
2026-03-15T01:53:30.6830596Z 1. Allocate raw memory using the game's heap (via the game's `operator new` at some address, or via the same mechanism plugin-sdk uses)
2026-03-15T01:53:30.6831224Z 2. Call the constructor at 0x65EAA0 on that memory
2026-03-15T01:53:30.6831436Z 
2026-03-15T01:53:30.6831631Z OR: use the raw function pointer approach with the game's `operator new`:
2026-03-15T01:53:30.6831986Z ```cpp
2026-03-15T01:53:30.6832197Z // Game's operator new at 0x82280A or similar
2026-03-15T01:53:30.6832492Z typedef void* (__cdecl* GameNew)(size_t);
2026-03-15T01:53:30.6832920Z static const GameNew gameNew = reinterpret_cast<GameNew>(0x82280A); // need correct address
2026-03-15T01:53:30.6833358Z void* rawMem = gameNew(0x4C);
2026-03-15T01:53:30.6833586Z ```
2026-03-15T01:53:30.6833684Z 
2026-03-15T01:53:30.6835977Z So the existing code uses `new CTaskComplexEnterCarAsDriver(targetCar)` which goes through plugin-sdk's wrapper that internally calls the game's constructor and allocates via the game's allocator, but since we don't have the header for CTaskComplexGangFollower, we'll need to manually allocate raw memory and invoke the constructor via function pointer instead. I should look for how plugin-sdk typically wraps memory allocation—there's usually something like `CMemoryMgr::Malloc` or a direct hook into the game's allocator.
2026-03-15T01:53:30.6837789Z 
2026-03-15T01:53:30.6841123Z Actually, the simplest path might be to find an existing game function that already assigns the follower task—like the CLEO opcode 0850 implementation—rather than trying to construct the task object ourselves. Allocating memory through the game's allocator via plugin-sdk and then directly calling the CTaskComplexGangFollower constructor at its hardcoded address to properly initialize the task structure. After construction, the memory will have the correct vtable set up, so casting it to `CTask*` gives us a valid object that the game can properly delete through its own allocator. Since plugin-sdk overrides `operator new` to use the game's memory manager, the destructor chain will work correctly when the game calls `delete`.
2026-03-15T01:53:30.6843777Z 
2026-03-15T01:53:30.6844573Z The challenge is we don't have plugin-sdk headers for `CTaskComplexGangFollower`, so I need to manually allocate the required 0x4C bytes from the game's heap and invoke the constructor through a function pointer instead of using the typed constructor.

The actual task assignment happens later in `CPedGroupIntelligence::Process()`, so by the time our POST_FOLLOW_CHECK fires 3 frames later, the task may have already been set and then cleared or changed again.
2026-03-15T01:54:47.2526434Z 
2026-03-15T01:54:47.2529650Z Or maybe 0x5F88D0 isn't `ComputeDefaultTasks` at all. I need to verify this address against the gta-reversed repository, particularly looking for functions in CPedGroupIntelligence that handle default task allocation like `TellMembersToFollowLeader`, `AllocateDefaultTasks`, or `UpdateDefaultTasks`. The commit c1bd664 mentioned that 0x5F88D0 applies an allocator type right after `SetDefaultTaskAllocatorType(0x5FBB70)`, so I should confirm whether this address actually does what we think it does.

2026-03-15T01:54:54.3399166Z     bool CPedGroupIntelligence::AddEvent(CEvent* event) {
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3399567Z         const auto eGrpEvent = notsa::dyn_cast<CEventGroupEvent>(event);
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3399918Z         if (!eGrpEvent) {
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3400143Z             return false;
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3400629Z         }
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3401039Z         if (!eGrpEvent->AffectsPedGroup(m_pPedGroup) || !eGrpEvent->GetEvent().AffectsPedGroup(m_pPedGroup)) {
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3401512Z             return false;

2026-03-15T01:54:54.3401985Z         if (const auto src = eGrpEvent->GetEvent().GetSourceEntity()) {

2026-03-15T01:54:54.3402865Z                 return false;
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3403086Z             }
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3403270Z         }

2026-03-15T01:54:54.3404544Z             eEditableResponse->ComputeResponseTaskType(m_pPedGroup);
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3404940Z             if (!eEditableResponse->WillRespond()) {
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3405241Z                 return false;
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3405460Z             }
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3405636Z         }

2026-03-15T01:54:54.3407448Z         return true;

2026-03-15T01:54:54.3424197Z     
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3424383Z         bool hasMemberListChanged{};

2026-03-15T01:54:54.3425211Z             if (m_PedTaskPairs[i].Ped == mem) {
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3425484Z                 continue;
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3425690Z             }
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3425900Z             m_PedTaskPairs[i].Ped          = mem;
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3426203Z             m_SecondaryPedTaskPairs[i].Ped = mem;
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3426505Z             m_DefaultPedTaskPairs[i].Ped   = mem;
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3426813Z             hasMemberListChanged             = true;
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3427084Z         }
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3427284Z         if (hasMemberListChanged) {
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3427584Z             if (m_PrimaryTaskAllocator) { // 0x5FC616
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3427921Z                 m_PrimaryTaskAllocator->AllocateTasks(this);
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3428380Z             } else { // 0x5FC620
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3428645Z                 ComputeDefaultTasks(nullptr);
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3428946Z                 ComputeScriptCommandTasks();
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3438844Z                 if (m_CurrentEvent) { // 0x5FC659
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3439264Z                     if (m_EventResponseTaskAllocator) { // 0x5FC65E
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3439692Z                         FlushTasks(m_PedTaskPairs, nullptr);
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3440070Z                         m_EventResponseTaskAllocator->AllocateTasks(this);
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3440594Z                     } else {
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3440932Z                         m_EventResponseTaskAllocator = ComputeEventResponseTasks();
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3441299Z                     }
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3441512Z                 }
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3441697Z             }
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3441875Z         }
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3442043Z     
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3442226Z         CEvent* eventToAdd{};
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3442500Z         if (m_HighestPriorityEvent) { // 0x5FC6A2
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3442855Z             if (ShouldSetHighestPriorityEventAsCurrent()) {

2026-03-15T01:54:54.3444647Z                         m_PrimaryTaskAllocator = nullptr;
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3444936Z                     }
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3445135Z                 }
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3445503Z                 delete std::exchange(m_EventResponseTaskAllocator, ComputeEventResponseTasks());
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3445938Z             } else if ( // 0x5FC6F6
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3446402Z                    m_HighestPriorityEvent->GetEvent().GetEventType() == m_CurrentEvent->GetEvent().GetEventType()
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3447010Z                 && m_HighestPriorityEvent->GetEvent().CanBeInterruptedBySameEvent()
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3447384Z             ) {
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3447637Z                 eventToAdd = m_HighestPriorityEvent->Clone();
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3447944Z             }
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3448129Z         }
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3448296Z     
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3448640Z         if (m_PrimaryTaskAllocator && !m_PrimaryTaskAllocator->ProcessGroup(this)) { // 0x5FC770
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3449141Z             delete std::exchange(m_PrimaryTaskAllocator, nullptr);
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3449453Z         }
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3449621Z

2026-03-15T01:54:54.3450569Z             delete std::exchange(m_HighestPriorityEvent, nullptr);
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3450889Z         }
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3451055Z     
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3451303Z         if (!m_PrimaryTaskAllocator && m_CurrentEvent) { // 0x5FC7A7
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3451668Z             if (m_EventResponseTaskAllocator) {

2026-03-15T01:54:54.3452498Z             }
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3452676Z         }
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3452845Z     
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3453022Z         if (eventToAdd) { // 0x5FC7C6
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3453280Z             AddEvent(eventToAdd);
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3453509Z         }
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3453697Z         delete eventToAdd;
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3453908Z     }

2026-03-15T01:54:54.3478229Z     // separated this out for readability @ 0x5FC6A2
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3478650Z     bool CPedGroupIntelligence::ShouldSetHighestPriorityEventAsCurrent() {
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3479058Z         assert(m_HighestPriorityEvent);
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3479311Z     
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3479496Z         // No current event?
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3479759Z         if (!m_CurrentEvent) {
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3479989Z             return true;
2026-03-15T01:54:54.3480307Z         }

2026-03-15T01:58:05.7846427Z     113.

2026-03-15T01:58:05.7853635Z     138.

2026-03-15T01:58:05.7854758Z     141.     float     bestDist = bestSnapped ? bestSnappedD : bestAnyD;

2026-03-15T01:58:05.7905269Z     262.             (int)ap.m_nCruiseSpeed, dest.x, dest.y, dest.z);
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7905581Z     263.         break;

2026-03-15T01:58:05.7906620Z     267.     case DriveMode::PARADO:
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7906856Z     268.     {

2026-03-15T01:58:05.7908977Z     275.     }

2026-03-15T01:58:05.7910023Z     279.         break;
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7910325Z     280.     }
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7910499Z     281. }
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7910663Z     282. 
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7910992Z     283. // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7911317Z     284. // ProcessDrivingAI
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7911722Z     285. // Per-frame: zonas STOP/SLOW, offroad, recovery, speed adaptativa,
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7912177Z     286. // auto-degrade CIVICO_D, periodic road snap, dump AI throttled.
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7912673Z     287. // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7913024Z     288. void ProcessDrivingAI(CPlayerPed* player)
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7913306Z     289. {
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7913496Z     290.     if (!IsCarValid()) return;
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7913747Z     291. 
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7913934Z     292.     CVehicle*   veh       = g_car;
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7914238Z     293.     CAutoPilot& ap        = veh->m_autoPilot;
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7914565Z     294.     CVector     vPos      = veh->GetPosition();
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7914910Z     295.     CVector     playerPos = player->GetPosition();
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7915265Z     296.     float       dist      = Dist2D(vPos, playerPos);
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7915554Z     297. 
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7915898Z     298.     // ── ZONA STOP: recruta completamente parado ──────────────────
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7916269Z     299.     if (dist < STOP_ZONE_M)
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7916509Z     300.     {
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7916708Z     301.         ap.m_nCruiseSpeed = 0;
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7917007Z     302.         ap.m_nCarMission  = MISSION_STOP_FOREVER;
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7917375Z     303.         return;
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7917575Z     304.     }
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7917747Z     305. 
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7918079Z     306.     // ── ZONA SLOW: recruta abranda + restaura missao CIVICO ──────
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7918730Z     307.     // A STOP zone pode ter sobrescrito mission=STOP_FOREVER(11).
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7919157Z     308.     // A SLOW zone restaura a missao CIVICO para que o road-follow
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7919578Z     309.     // retome assim que o carro sair da zona (dist > SLOW_ZONE_M).
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7919917Z     310.     if (dist < SLOW_ZONE_M)
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7920150Z     311.     {
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7920689Z     312.         ap.m_nCruiseSpeed = SPEED_SLOW;
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7921007Z     313.         if (IsCivicoMode(g_driveMode))
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7921268Z     314.         {
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7921551Z     315.             eCarMission expectedM = GetExpectedMission(g_driveMode);
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7921960Z     316.             if (ap.m_nCarMission != expectedM)
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7922238Z     317.             {
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7922564Z     318.                 CVehicle* pCar = player->bInVehicle ? player->m_pVehicle : nullptr;
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7922976Z     319.                 if (!g_slowZoneRestoring)
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7923246Z     320.                 {
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7923570Z     321.                     LogDrive("SLOW_ZONE: dist=%.1fm missao_atual=%d->%s restaurada "
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7924064Z     322.                              "speed=%d targetCar=%s (road-follow retomara ao sair da zona)",
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7924484Z     323.                         dist, (int)ap.m_nCarMission,
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7924950Z     324.                         (expectedM == MISSION_43) ? "MISSION_43(EscortRear)" : "MISSION_34(FollowFaraway)",
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7925401Z     325.                         (int)SPEED_SLOW,
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7925715Z     326.                         pCar ? "valido" : "nullptr(pe)");
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7926050Z     327.                     g_slowZoneRestoring = true;
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7926324Z     328.                 }
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7926665Z     329.                 ap.m_nCarMission = expectedM;
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7926978Z     330.                 if (pCar) ap.m_pTargetCar = pCar;
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7927257Z     331.             }
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7927446Z     332.         }
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7927634Z     333.         return;
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7927830Z     334.     }
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7928003Z     335. 
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7928273Z     336.     // Saiu da SLOW_ZONE (dist >= SLOW_ZONE_M): road-follow retomado.
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7928856Z     337.     // NOTA: o re-snap via JoinCarWithRoadSystem foi REMOVIDO daqui porque
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7929368Z     338.     // produzia linkId invalido (0xFFFFFE1E) quando o carro estava numa posicao
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7929967Z     339.     // critica (interseccao, passeio, etc.) → activava o guard INVALID_LINK →
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7930658Z     340.     // recruta beelining no passeio por ate ~28 segundos.
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7931100Z     341.     // O snap periodico (ROAD_SNAP_INTERVAL=300fr) e suficiente para re-alinhar.
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7931501Z     342.     if (g_slowZoneRestoring)
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7931744Z     343.     {
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7931952Z     344.         g_slowZoneRestoring = false;
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7932509Z     345.         LogDrive("SLOW_ZONE: saiu (dist=%.1fm) — road-follow retomado (snap periodico em %d frames)",
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7933027Z     346.             dist, ROAD_SNAP_INTERVAL - g_civicRoadSnapTimer);
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7933336Z     347.     }
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7933507Z     348. 
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7933841Z     349.     // ── Verificacao de offroad (throttled) ───────────────────────
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7934191Z     350.     if (g_offroadTimer <= 0)
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7934430Z     351.     {
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7934636Z     352.         bool wasOffroad = g_isOffroad;
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7935022Z     353.         g_isOffroad     = DetectOffroad(veh);
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7935341Z     354.         g_offroadTimer  = OFFROAD_CHECK_INTERVAL;
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7935657Z     355.         if (g_isOffroad != wasOffroad)
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7935974Z     356.         {
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7936214Z     357.             LogDrive("Offroad: %s -> %s (modo=%s)",
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7936526Z     358.                 wasOffroad ? "SIM" : "NAO",
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7936824Z     359.                 g_isOffroad  ? "SIM" : "NAO",
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7937124Z     360.                 DriveModeName(g_driveMode));
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7937498Z     361.             // Ao entrar em offroad em modo CIVICO: snap imediato ao road-graph
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7937943Z     362.             // para tentar re-alinhar com a estrada (sem beelining).
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7938332Z     363.             if (g_isOffroad && IsCivicoMode(g_driveMode))
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7938627Z     364.             {
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7938884Z     365.                 CCarCtrl::JoinCarWithRoadSystem(veh);
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7939210Z     366.                 g_civicRoadSnapTimer = 0;
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7939828Z     367.                 LogDrive("OFFROAD_ENTER_SNAP: JoinCarWithRoadSystem ao detectar offroad (CIVICO — sem beelining)");
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7940457Z     368.             }
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7940647Z     369.         }
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7940829Z     370.     }
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7941006Z     371.     else
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7941189Z     372.     {
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7941382Z     373.         --g_offroadTimer;
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7941615Z     374.     }
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7941793Z     375. 
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7942151Z     376.     // ── Offroad + CIVICO: impedir snap periodico extra, sem beelining ─
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7942629Z     377.     // Em CIVICO o recruta deve seguir pela estrada mesmo quando offroad.
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7943097Z     378.     // GOTOCOORDS+PLOUGH_THROUGH (beelining) e apenas para DIRETO.
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7943587Z     379.     // Mantemos g_civicRoadSnapTimer=0 para forcar re-snap ao sair de offroad.
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7944030Z     380.     if (g_isOffroad && IsCivicoMode(g_driveMode))
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7944391Z     381.     {
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7944602Z     382.         g_civicRoadSnapTimer = 0;
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7944853Z     383.     }
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7945028Z     384. 
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7945367Z     385.     // ── Modo DIRETO: actualizar destino periodicamente ───────────
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7945753Z     386.     if (g_driveMode == DriveMode::DIRETO)
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7946020Z     387.     {
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7946221Z     388.         if (g_diretoTimer <= 0)
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7946466Z     389.         {
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7946772Z     390.             ap.m_vecDestinationCoors = playerPos;
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7947115Z     391.             g_diretoTimer = DIRETO_UPDATE_INTERVAL;
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7947399Z     392.         }
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7947585Z     393.         else
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7947785Z     394.         {
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7947985Z     395.             --g_diretoTimer;
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7948225Z     396.         }
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7948456Z     397.         ap.m_nCruiseSpeed     = SPEED_DIRETO;
2026-03-15T01:58:05.7948817Z     398.         ap.m_nCarDrivingStyle = DRIVINGSTYLE_PLOUGH_THROUGH;

I'm seeing the core issue now—the respect boost is actually working (STAT_RESPECT = 1000 confirms it), but the user wants it removed because it's interfering with vanilla Grove Street recruitment mechanics. The persistent boost from ActivateRespectBoost/DeactivateRespectBoost is preventing the normal respect system from functioning as intended. Task 900 appears briefly in the sequence and seems to be TASK_COMPLEX_GANG_FOLLOWER based on the task type values, which would make sense as the system attempts to transition the NPC into following the player after the spawn sequence completes. failing the Respects check. The real issue is why GANG_FOLLOWER never actually starts—my theory is that `TellGroupToStartFollowingPlayer` assigns the task to each group member, but `m_PedTaskPairs[0].Ped` might not be properly initialized yet. 2026-03-15T02:00:57.2175250Z 29:  respeito: STAT_RESPECT=68 lido por CPedIntelligence::Respects (0x601C90)
2026-03-15T02:00:57.2175823Z ```
2026-03-15T02:00:57.2175927Z 
2026-03-15T02:00:57.2176938Z So `CPedIntelligence::Respects` at 0x601C90 is used to check if a ped will follow. And the comment says "STAT_RESPECT=68 lido" - meaning the current STAT_RESPECT at time of writing was 68. But in the log body: `respect=1000` due to ActivateRespectBoost.
2026-03-15T02:00:57.2178229Z 
2026-03-15T02:00:57.2179402Z But WAIT: at spawn time (frame 573), the ped's position is `(2490.9,-1669.5,13.3)` and it NEVER moves - even with `STAT_RESPECT = 1000`! The tasks always show `[4]STAND_STILL(203)`. GANG_FOLLOWER is never attempted! Not even task 900 shows up at earlier frames...
2026-03-15T02:00:57.2180963Z  
2026-03-15T02:00:57.2182290Z I'm checking what task 900 represents in the task type definitions - it doesn't appear to be a standard task, so it might be part of the gang spawn initialization sequence. The critical finding is that the ped never receives the GANG_FOLLOWER task despite having maximum respect, which explains why it remains stationary.

2026-03-15T02:01:23.8963238Z copilot: Now I have the full picture. Let me look at Main.cpp to see what happens with keys, the main loop, and the shared globals.
2026-03-15T02:01:23.8964098Z 
2026-03-15T02:01:23.8964309Z Then I need to understand two key things from the gta-reversed source:
2026-03-15T02:01:23.8964821Z 1. What does `CPedIntelligence::Respects(0x601C90)` actually check?
2026-03-15T02:01:23.8965266Z 2. What does `TellGroupToStartFollowingPlayer` do exactly?
2026-03-15T02:01:23.8965515Z 
2026-03-15T02:01:23.8965618Z From the comments in the code:
2026-03-15T02:01:23.8966205Z ```// respeito: STAT_RESPECT=68 lido por CPedIntelligence::Respects (0x601C90)```
2026-03-15T02:01:23.8966782Z 
2026-03-15T02:01:23.8966962Z And from the ActivateRespectBoost:
2026-03-15T02:01:23.8967479Z 
2026-03-15T02:01:23.8967493Z 
2026-03-15T02:01:23.8971398Z The respect boost is being checked periodically by `FindMaxNumberOfGroupMembers` to validate group slots — if the boost wears off, it either recruits or removes members. But `TellGroupToStartFollowingPlayer` creates a `CTaskComplexGangFollower` whose first subtask runs on the next frame through the task manager, which is after the temporary boost has already been restored. This is why a persistent boost is needed for the respect check to work. However, the user is saying this boost is breaking vanilla Grove recruitment and wants it removed entirely.
2026-03-15T02:01:23.8974543Z 
2026-03-15T02:01:23.8975956Z Let me check the gta-reversed source to see what `Respects()` at 0x601C90 actually does: Looking at the function, I can see it's checking the player's respect value directly without any boost applied.

2026-03-15T02:02:15.9096664Z copilot: Very important! Looking at `TellGroupToStartFollowingPlayer`:
2026-03-15T02:02:15.9097221Z 
2026-03-15T02:02:15.9097367Z ```cpp
2026-03-15T02:02:15.9098008Z void CPlayerPed::TellGroupToStartFollowingPlayer(bool arg0, bool arg1, bool arg2) {
2026-03-15T02:02:15.9098752Z     ...
2026-03-15T02:02:15.9099419Z     groupIntel.SetDefaultTaskAllocatorType(ePedGroupDefaultTaskAllocatorType::RANDOM);
2026-03-15T02:02:15.9100416Z     if (arg0) {
2026-03-15T02:02:15.9100833Z         CEventPlayerCommandToGroup playerCmdEvent;
2026-03-15T02:02:15.9101939Z         playerCmdEvent.ComputeResponseTaskType(&group);
2026-03-15T02:02:15.9102534Z         if (playerCmdEvent.WillRespond()) {
2026-03-15T02:02:15.9103310Z             auto gatherCmdEvent = new CEventPlayerCommandToGroup(ePlayerGroupCommand::PLAYER_GROUP_COMMAND_GATHER);
2026-03-15T02:02:15.9104062Z 
2026-03-15T02:02:15.9104066Z 
2026-03-15T02:02:15.9104146Z ```
2026-03-15T02:02:15.9104246Z 
2026-03-15T02:02:15.9104349Z Continuing with the event setup...
2026-03-15T02:02:15.9104603Z ```
2026-03-15T02:02:15.9104802Z 
2026-03-15T02:02:15.9106878Z The key insight is that `TellGroupToStartFollowingPlayer` doesn't directly set the task allocator to GANG_FOLLOWER—it sets it to RANDOM and fires an event instead. The event system then processes this through the group intelligence, where `WillRespond()` checks the respect/relationship values to determine if members actually follow, ultimately deciding the task type based on those relationships.
2026-03-15T02:02:15.9108778Z 
2026-03-15T02:02:15.9109613Z The real bottleneck is that `FindMaxGroupMembers()` reads the player's STAT_RESPECT to determine how many group slots are available. With low respect, this returns 0, which causes the RESCAN to detect an empty slot and dismiss the group entirely.
2026-03-15T02:02:15.9110761Z 
2026-03-15T02:02:15.9110843Z ```
2026-03-15T02:02:15.9113880Z So the respect boost has two critical effects: it enables the `WillRespond()` check that assigns GANG_FOLLOWER status, and it determines the maximum group size through `FindMaxGroupMembers()`. But the user is saying the persistent respect boost at 1000 is actually breaking vanilla recruitment mechanics—something about how the game behaves differently at maximum respect that prevents the normal recruitment flow from working.
2026-03-15T02:02:15.9116460Z 
2026-03-15T02:02:15.9117348Z I need to figure out what's actually broken: either the GANG_FOLLOWER assignment is failing because the event system isn't firing correctly, or the group slot detection is getting confused by the artificially high respect value.
2026-03-15T02:02:15.9118517Z ```
2026-03-15T02:02:15.9121343Z Looking at the task allocator type being set to RANDOM—that might be allowing the ped to pick any behavior from a pool instead of staying locked into follow mode. In vanilla GTA SA, when you press N to make recruited NPCs follow, it calls `TellGroupToStartFollowingPlayer` with passive mode, which should put them into GANG_FOLLOWER. But I need to trace what actually happens in the vanilla follow command to see where our implementation diverges.
2026-03-15T02:02:15.9123254Z ```
2026-03-15T02:02:15.9125746Z I'm examining the CLEO opcode 0850 which directly instantiates CTaskComplexGangFollower on the ped, bypassing the group intelligence system entirely—that's why vanilla recruitment works. It skips the respect check and directly assigns the task to the ped's task manager, which is probably what we need to replicate.
2026-03-15T02:02:15.9127862Z ```
2026-03-15T02:02:15.9128320Z GroupIntelSetDefaultTaskAllocatorType(pIntel, 4)
2026-03-15T02:02:15.9128850Z ```
2026-03-15T02:02:15.9129154Z and
2026-03-15T02:02:15.9129432Z ```
2026-03-15T02:02:15.9129810Z GroupIntelComputeDefaultTasks(pIntel, g_recruit)
2026-03-15T02:02:15.9130500Z ```
2026-03-15T02:02:15.9131070Z might be the wrapper functions that handle this, so I should investigate whether these already do what we need or if we need to go lower level.
2026-03-15T02:02:15.9131762Z 
2026-03-15T02:02:15.9135525Z Looking at the SetDefaultTaskAllocator implementation, it clears existing tasks and then calls AllocateDefaultTasks with nullptr to apply to all peds in the group. The issue is that type 4 (FOLLOW_LEADER_ANY_MEANS) might still fail if the ped references aren't properly initialized first, so I need to trace through whether Process() gets called automatically or if we need to manually trigger it. I'm seeing that when `ped=nullptr` is passed to `GetPedsTaskPair`, it matches against the default task pair slot where `Ped` is also nullptr, which means the task gets written to an empty slot without an actual ped pointer attached. This looks intentional—`AllocateDefaultTasks` might handle the nullptr case differently, possibly as a way to set up default behavior for the group rather than individual peds.
2026-03-15T02:02:15.9139802Z 
2026-03-15T02:02:15.9141426Z Looking at the GTA SA reversed source code, I need to find the exact addresses for `CTaskComplexGangFollower` and the relevant `CTaskManager::SetTask` methods to directly assign the gang follower task to a ped's task manager instead of going through the default task allocator system.

2026-03-15T02:02:51.5614020Z Actually, I think the most critical issue is:
2026-03-15T02:02:51.5614416Z 
2026-03-15T02:02:51.5615234Z 1. `TellGroupToStartFollowingPlayer(aggressive=true, false, false)` calls `SetDefaultTaskAllocatorType(RANDOM)` and fires a `PLAYER_GROUP_COMMAND_GATHER` event.

2026-03-15T02:02:51.5616461Z 
2026-03-15T02:02:51.5616467Z 
2026-03-15T02:02:51.5617074Z 3. `WillRespond()` checks the ped's willingness to follow, but I need to trace through the actual implementation in gta-reversed.
2026-03-15T02:02:51.5617709Z 
2026-03-15T02:02:51.5619595Z Looking at the flow more carefully: `TellGroupToStartFollowingPlayer(true, false, false)` sends an event to the group that should eventually create a `GANG_FOLLOWER` task, but `WillRespond()` might be failing. 2026-03-15T02:02:51.5621339Z

2026-03-15T02:02:51.5623617Z 
2026-03-15T02:02:51.5624696Z So the actual logic continues after that check: the function sets up the group to enter the leader's vehicle if needed, configures the task allocator to random mode, and if the follow command is active, it creates a player command event and dispatches a gather command to the group through the event system.
2026-03-15T02:02:51.5625983Z 
2026-03-15T02:02:51.5628744Z Now I'm tracing through the early return condition in the follow function. We're calling with aggressive and two false flags, so if the group has no members besides the leader, it returns early. But looking at the logs from frame 692, there's a rescan happening and an attempt to recruit the ped into the group, which suggests the membership count might actually be zero at that point despite the ped being present. At frame 693, the task transitions from the spawn sequence to standing still, then cycles through some intermediate states before settling back on stand still. The key thing is that `TellGroupToStartFollowingPlayer` does trigger the gather behavior, which explains the task chain I'm seeing in the logs.

2026-03-15T02:03:07.3506597Z copilot: From gta-reversed, `CTaskComplexGangFollower::Constructor` is at `0x65EAA0`. The constructor signature is:
2026-03-15T02:03:07.3507279Z ```cpp
2026-03-15T02:03:07.3507789Z CTaskComplexGangFollower::CTaskComplexGangFollower(CPedGroup* pedGroup, CPed* ped, uint8 a4, CVector pos, float a6)
2026-03-15T02:03:07.3508338Z ```
2026-03-15T02:03:07.3508433Z 
2026-03-15T02:03:07.3509110Z Now I understand. The original code in `AddRecruitToGroup` ultimately calls `TellGroupToStartFollowingPlayer` which fires an event that creates `CTaskComplexGangFollower`. But this fails for the recruit.
2026-03-15T02:03:07.3509860Z 
2026-03-15T02:03:07.3511155Z **The KEY approach**: Instead of relying on `TellGroupToStartFollowingPlayer` (which uses the event system and can fail), we should DIRECTLY create and assign `CTaskComplexGangFollower` to the recruit's task manager. This is what CLEO's opcode 0850 does.
2026-03-15T02:03:07.3513006Z 
2026-03-15T02:03:07.3513011Z 
2026-03-15T02:03:07.3513617Z Now I need to locate `CTaskManager::SetTask` in the plugin-sdk to understand how to directly assign the task to the ped.

2026-03-15T02:03:12.6792388Z     # grove_recruit_asi — Análise CLEO vs plugin-sdk e Protótipo ASI
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6793029Z     
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6793412Z     ## O que é o plugin-sdk
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6794020Z     
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6795221Z     O [DK22Pac/plugin-sdk](https://github.com/DK22Pac/plugin-sdk) é um SDK C++ que expõe as classes internas do GTA SA como headers C++ compiláveis.  
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6797289Z     Permite criar ficheiros `.asi` (DLLs injectadas pelo ASI Loader) que correm como código nativo dentro do processo do jogo, com acesso total às estruturas internas.
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6798253Z     
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6798439Z     ---
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6798621Z     
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6798915Z     ## Comparação directa: CLEO vs plugin-sdk
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6799317Z     
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6799558Z     | Capacidade | CLEO (actual) | plugin-sdk (ASI) |
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6799882Z     |---|---|---|
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6800557Z     | **Frequência de actualização** | ~300 ms (wait mínimo do loop) | Per-frame (~16 ms a 60 fps) |
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6801896Z     | **Speed adaptativa para curvas** | ❌ Sem opcode para ler o multiplicador do link | ✅ `CCarCtrl::FindSpeedMultiplierWithSpeedFromNodes(m_nStraightLineDistance)` |
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6803241Z     | **Detecção automática de offroad** | ⚠️ Só com guard 00EC + 04D3 (manual, 300 ms) | ✅ `CCarCtrl::FindNodesThisCarIsNearestTo` per-frame |
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6804308Z     | **Alinhamento de faixa** | ⚠️ Só activo em DriveMode=Accurate (CIVICO-C) | ✅ `CCarCtrl::ClipTargetOrientationToLink` em qualquer modo |
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6805430Z     | **Controlo de velocidade** | ⚠️ `00AD` (float arredondado, aplicado pelo CLEO) | ✅ `CAutoPilot::m_nCruiseSpeed` (char, escrita directa, precisa) |
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6806354Z     | **Múltiplos recrutas** | ❌ 1 recruta (1 handle, 1 estado) | ✅ Até 7 via `PoolIterator<CPed>` |
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6807033Z     | **Leitura de CarMission** | ❌ Sem opcode | ✅ `CAutoPilot::m_nCarMission` |
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6807675Z     | **Detecção de highway** | ❌ Sem opcode | ✅ `CPathNode::m_bHighway` / `m_bNotHighway` |
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6808302Z     | **Nível de tráfego do nó** | ❌ Sem opcode | ✅ `CPathNode::m_nTrafficLevel` |
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6809424Z     | **Pathfinding completo** | ⚠️ 04D3 só o nó mais próximo | ✅ `CPathFind::DoPathSearch` (rota completa nó-a-nó) |
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6810688Z     | **Coexistência com CLEO** | — | ✅ Corre em paralelo, sem conflito |
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6811246Z

2026-03-15T02:03:12.6814128Z     Offset  Campo                    Tipo              Descrição
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6814977Z     0x14    m_nCurrentPathNodeInfo   CCarPathLinkAddress  Link actual (para ClipToLink e SpeedMult)
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6815885Z     0x27    m_nCurrentLane           char              Faixa actual
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6816793Z     0x29    m_nCarDrivingStyle       eCarDrivingStyle  0=StopForCars, 2=AvoidCars, 3=PloughThrough, 4=SFI
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6818138Z     0x2A    m_nCarMission            eCarMission       Missão actual (8=GotoCoords, 12=Accurate, 11=StopForever...)
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6819238Z     0x40    m_nCruiseSpeed           char              Velocidade de cruzeiro (escrita directa, ~km/h)
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6824792Z     0x4D    m_nStraightLineDistance  char              Distância em linha recta ao nó seguinte
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6825817Z     0x5C    m_vecDestinationCoors    CVector           Destino actual da IA
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6826695Z     0x8C    m_pTargetCar             CVehicle*         Carro-alvo (para EscortRear/FollowFar)
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6827090Z     ```
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6827412Z     
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6827749Z     ### `eCarMission` — `plugin_sa/game_sa/eCarMission.h`
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6828285Z     Missões relevantes para o nosso mod:
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6828574Z     - `MISSION_NONE = 0`
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6829028Z     - `MISSION_GOTOCOORDS = 8` — equivalente ao 00A7 do CLEO
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6829709Z     - `MISSION_GOTOCOORDS_ACCURATE = 12` — equivalente ao 02C2 do CLEO
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6830587Z     - `MISSION_STOP_FOREVER = 11` — parar completamente
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6831318Z     - `MISSION_34 = 52` — FollowCarFaraway (06E1 CIVICO-E)
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6831889Z     - `MISSION_43 = 67` — EscortRearFaraway (06E1 CIVICO-D)
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6832179Z     
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6832643Z     ### `eCarDrivingStyle` — `plugin_sa/game_sa/CAutoPilot.h`
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6832964Z     ```cpp
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6833619Z     DRIVINGSTYLE_STOP_FOR_CARS              = 0   // pára para carros, respeita semáforos
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6834199Z     DRIVINGSTYLE_SLOW_DOWN_FOR_CARS         = 1
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6834756Z     DRIVINGSTYLE_AVOID_CARS                 = 2   // desvia, ignora semáforos (CIVICO-D/E/DIRETO)
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6835536Z     DRIVINGSTYLE_PLOUGH_THROUGH             = 3   // ignora tudo (offroad)
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6836336Z     DRIVINGSTYLE_STOP_FOR_CARS_IGNORE_LIGHTS= 4   // pára obstáculos, ignora semáforos (CIVICO-0/A/B/C/F)
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6837033Z     ```
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6837208Z     
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6837604Z     ### `CCarCtrl` — `plugin_sa/game_sa/CCarCtrl.h`
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6837923Z     
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6838266Z     **`FindNodesThisCarIsNearestTo(CVehicle*, CNodeAddress&, CNodeAddress&)`**  
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6838909Z     Encontra os 2 nós de estrada mais próximos ao carro.  
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6839567Z     Se o nó mais próximo está a >30 m → carro está fora de estrada (canal, montanha).  
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6840150Z     CLEO não tem equivalente directo.
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6840798Z     
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6841358Z     **`ClipTargetOrientationToLink(CVehicle*, CCarPathLinkAddress, char lane, float* heading, float fwdX, float fwdY)`**  
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6842258Z     Ajusta `heading` para ser paralelo à faixa de estrada do link actual.  
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6842990Z     Internamente activo só em `DriveMode=Accurate`. Via ASI aplicamos a qualquer modo.  
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6843728Z     Resultado: recruta não corta curvas, mantém-se na faixa correcta.
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6844062Z     
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6853143Z     **`FindSpeedMultiplierWithSpeedFromNodes(char straightLineDist)`**  
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6853904Z     Lê o limite de velocidade do link para a curva actual.  
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6854562Z     Retorna `float [0.0, 1.0]`: 1.0 = reta, <1.0 = curva (proporcional ao ângulo).  
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6855206Z     Permite pre-abrandar antes da curva — impossível em CLEO.
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6855540Z     
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6855837Z     **`StopCarIfNodesAreInvalid(CVehicle*)`**  
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6856372Z     Para o carro imediatamente se os nós do seu AutoPilot estão inválidos.  
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6856966Z     Útil como guard de segurança quando offroad.
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6857252Z     
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6857655Z     ### `CPathNode` — `plugin_sa/game_sa/CPathNode.h`
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6857945Z     ```
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6858420Z     m_bHighway          — nó de auto-estrada (permitir velocidades mais altas)
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6858914Z     m_bNotHighway       — explicitamente não-highway
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6859436Z     m_nTrafficLevel     — densidade de tráfego [0-3]
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6859871Z     m_bWaterNode        — nó aquático (recruta nunca deve navegar aqui)
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6860793Z     m_bDontWander       — nó que NPCs civis evitam
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6861248Z     m_nNumLinks         — número de ligações do nó
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6861626Z     ```
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6861805Z     
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6862427Z     ### `CPools` / `PoolIterator` — `plugin_sa/game_sa/CPools.h` + `shared/extensions/PoolIterator.h`
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6862937Z     ```cpp
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6863298Z     // Iterar todos os peds activos no jogo (range-based for):
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6863666Z     for (CPed* ped : *CPools::ms_pPedPool) {
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6864206Z         // ped é garantidamente válido dentro do loop

2026-03-15T02:03:12.6864985Z     ```
2026-03-15T02:03:12.6865386Z     CLEO não tem equivalente: a varredura manual com `0A8D` é propensa a crashes.

2026-03-15T02:04:05.9043861Z 
2026-03-15T02:04:05.9044541Z copilot: EUREKA! This is the critical piece. The `Respects` function at 0x601C90:
2026-03-15T02:04:05.9045140Z 
2026-03-15T02:04:05.9045485Z ```cpp
2026-03-15T02:04:05.9045908Z bool CPedIntelligence::Respects(CPed* ped) const {
2026-03-15T02:04:05.9046525Z     auto respect = m_pPed->GetAcquaintance().GetRespect();
2026-03-15T02:04:05.9047193Z     auto pedFlag = CPedType::GetPedFlag(ped->m_nPedType);
2026-03-15T02:04:05.9047896Z     return pedFlag & respect;
2026-03-15T02:04:05.9048282Z }```
2026-03-15T02:04:05.9048440Z 
2026-03-15T02:04:05.9050670Z This uses `m_pPed->GetAcquaintance().GetRespect()` - this is NOT `STAT_RESPECT`! It's the ped's acquaintance system which maps relationship bits. `GetRespect()` returns a bitmask of ped types that THIS ped "respects" (follows commands from). `pedFlag` is a bitmask corresponding to the player ped's type (PEDTYPE_PLAYER1 = 0?).
2026-03-15T02:04:05.9052633Z 
2026-03-15T02:04:05.9052639Z 
2026-03-15T02:04:05.9055478Z So the recruit ped (GSF, pedType=8) needs to have the PLAYER ped type in its "respect" acquaintance bitmask. This is a **relationship** check, not a stats check. Our `ActivateRespectBoost` sets `STAT_RESPECT` (the player's reputation stat), but `CPedIntelligence::Respects` checks the ped's ACQUAINTANCE SYSTEM (relationship flags), which is completely different! The respect boost has been wrong from the start - `STAT_RESPECT` is for determining group size limits, not for making peds follow commands.
2026-03-15T02:04:05.9058662Z 
2026-03-15T02:04:05.9061144Z `CPedAcquaintance::GetRespect()` returns a bitmask of ped types that this ped respects and will follow, and for a GSF NPC that's likely defined in the game's relationship data. The vanilla CLEO recruit opcode bypasses the Respects check entirely by directly creating the follower task, which is why it works - but our ASI mod needs to actually set the acquaintance relationship properly.
2026-03-15T02:04:05.9063484Z 
2026-03-15T02:04:05.9064921Z I'm looking at two approaches: either add the player's ped type to the recruit's respect bitmask, or bypass the Respects check altogether by directly creating the follower task like CLEO does. The second approach seems cleaner since it mirrors the working CLEO implementation.
2026-03-15T02:04:05.9066654Z 
2026-03-15T02:04:05.9068487Z Actually, I realize there's a simpler solution—I can just fix the Respects check by setting the recruit's acquaintance to respect the player before calling TellGroupToStartFollowingPlayer, so let me search for the GetAcquaintance method.
2026-03-15T02:04:05.9069398Z 
2026-03-15T02:04:05.9070916Z Looking into how GTA SA handles ped relationships through the acquaintance system, I need to find where SetAcquaintance is defined or how the respect relationship gets set—the CLEO opcode 0621 sets this, so I should be able to replicate that in ASI by directly manipulating the acquaintance bits.

2026-03-15T02:04:15.1801553Z     enum {
2026-03-15T02:04:15.1801853Z         ACQUAINTANCE_RESPECT = 0,
2026-03-15T02:04:15.1802484Z         ACQUAINTANCE_LIKE    = 1,
2026-03-15T02:04:15.1802886Z         ACQUAINTANCE_IGNORE  = 2,
2026-03-15T02:04:15.1803306Z         ACQUAINTANCE_DISLIKE = 3,
2026-03-15T02:04:15.1803734Z         ACQUAINTANCE_HATE    = 4,
2026-03-15T02:04:15.1804112Z     
2026-03-15T02:04:15.1804405Z         ACQUAINTANCE_NUM
2026-03-15T02:04:15.1804743Z     };
2026-03-15T02:04:15.1805019Z     
2026-03-15T02:04:15.1805679Z     // This structure stores information about how a ped of given pedtype (each entry in PedRelationship[32]
2026-03-15T02:04:15.1807004Z     // is separate pedtype, starting from 0 (PLAYER1) to 31 (MISSION8)) behaves toward any ped of other type.
2026-03-15T02:04:15.1808032Z     // Five dwords in this structure (only four of them are used) are the bitmasks,
2026-03-15T02:04:15.1808901Z     // where a bit sets a flag of acquaintance type (respect, like, dislike, hate)
2026-03-15T02:04:15.1809738Z     // to the pedtype of given index (=number of bit).
2026-03-15T02:04:15.1810676Z     // Say, if the 4th bit (counting from zero) in PedRelationship[5].respect is set,
2026-03-15T02:04:15.1811755Z     // it means that a ped of type CIVFEMALE (pedtype=5) respects a ped of type CIVMALE (pedtype=4).
2026-03-15T02:04:15.1812494Z     //
2026-03-15T02:04:15.1812945Z     // These structures are filled with data from the file ped.dat.
2026-03-15T02:04:15.1813737Z     // The relationship flags could be changed using opcode 0746 and similar.
2026-03-15T02:04:15.1814365Z     //
2026-03-15T02:04:15.1814851Z     // https://gtamods.com/wiki/Saves_(GTA_SA)#Block_19:_Ped_Relationships
2026-03-15T02:04:15.1815469Z     class CAcquaintance {

2026-03-15T02:04:15.1816141Z         union {

2026-03-15T02:04:15.1818792Z             };
